label: I'm more sensitive to sudden than steady noises.

bipolarLabels.left: Dim, warm, and atmospheric (e.g., low lamps)
bipolarLabels.right: Bright, cool, and fully illuminated (e.g., overhead lights)

label: During the evening, what is your preferred lighting environment in the living room?

bipolarLabels.right: Always play audio aloud through the phone speaker

label: When consuming media (TikTok, Instagram, voice notes) in common areas, what is your standard behavior?
If we are both working from home, what is your expectation for the general acoustic environment of the apartment?

options.label: Library rules: Absolute silence is required
Cafe vibe: Low-level background noise or quiet chatting is fine
Normal living: I can tune out normal household activity

label: I'm comfortable when living-room conversations are faintly audible in my room.
I find it highly distracting if two audio sources are playing simultaneously (e.g., the TV is on, and someone is watching a video on their phone).
I expect a quiet environment during standard working hours (9-to-5) to accommodate working from home.
Do you prefer having the TV or music playing continuously as 'background noise' throughout the day, even if you aren't actively paying attention to it?
I frequently cook meals that involve deep frying, strong spices, or ingredients that leave lingering odors in the kitchen for hours.
If your housemate is playing music slightly too loud while you are trying to read, how do you handle it?

options.label: I immediately ask them to turn it down
I put on my own headphones or move to another room
I wait to see if it stops, but feel increasingly frustrated

label: I regularly use scented candles, incense, or strong room sprays to alter the smell of the apartment.
Even if a space is technically clean, having too many personal items or visual clutter left out in the living room prevents me from relaxing.
Immediately after returning home from a long workday, I require at least 30 minutes of total silence before engaging in conversation or household activities.

bipolarLabels.left: Keep the heating low and wear thick sweaters
bipolarLabels.right: Keep the heating high enough to wear t-shirts indoors

label: During the winter months, how do you prefer to manage the indoor climate?
Do you strongly prefer keeping windows open for fresh air, even if it means the apartment gets noticeably cold or drafts occur?
I make a highly conscious effort to walk softly and close doors gently if I know someone is resting or working in the next room.
In the common areas, do you prefer keeping the blinds/curtains wide open for maximum sunlight, or drawn closed for maximum privacy?
How do you prefer lighting in shared spaces?
I am easily distracted from my work or relaxation if I can hear muffled conversation from another room.
I'm comfortable with loud media, lively dinners, or voice calls in the living room during the evening before quiet hours.
I regularly watch TV or listen to music without headphones in my bedroom late at night, assuming the walls will block the sound.
Do you play a musical instrument at home?

options.label: No, I do not play any instruments
Yes, but exclusively with headphones (e.g., electric keyboard/guitar)
Yes, an acoustic/amplified instrument, and I expect to practice during the day

label: I feel comfortable taking work or personal calls from the shared living room while my housemate is home.
What is your approach to running loud appliances (washing machines, blenders, vacuums)?

options.label: I run them whenever it is convenient for my schedule
I try to run them only when my housemate is awake, but I don't strictly plan around them
I strictly coordinate appliance use to ensure it doesn't disturb my housemate's rest or WFH schedule

label: How would you describe your natural speaking volume and expressiveness when relaxing at home?
